result.summary: Mermaid syntax or branch coverage was incomplete.
result.rawLog:
model=gemma-4-31B-it
scenario=SO-11 Natural Language to Mermaid Diagram
user_turn_1=Generate a Mermaid flowchart for this process: User submits a form. System validates the input. If valid, save to database and send confirmation email, then show success page. If invalid, show error message and return to form.
assistant_turn_1=graph TD
    A[User submits form] --> B{System validates input}
    B -- Valid --> C[Save to database]
    C --> D[Send confirmation email]
    D --> E[Show success page]
    B -- Invalid --> F[Show error message]
    F --> G[Return to form]
final_answer=graph TD
    A[User submits form] --> B{System validates input}
    B -- Valid --> C[Save to database]
    C --> D[Send confirmation email]
    D --> E[Show success page]
    B -- Invalid --> F[Show error message]
    F --> G[Return to form]
validator_status=fail
validator_summary=Mermaid syntax or branch coverage was incomplete.
verdict=fail
score=25
summary=Mermaid syntax or branch coverage was incomplete.

result.summary: TOML package metadata and dependencies were valid.
result.rawLog:
model=gemma-4-31B-it
scenario=SO-04 TOML Configuration
user_turn_1=Generate a TOML configuration file for a Rust project with: package name "my_cli", version "0.1.0", edition "2021", authors list with "Alice <alice@example.com>". Dependencies section: serde version "1.0" with features ["derive"], clap version "4.5".
assistant_turn_1=[package]
name = "my_cli"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"
authors = ["Alice <alice@example.com>"]

[dependencies]
serde = { version = "1.0", features = ["derive"] }
clap = "4.5"
final_answer=[package]
name = "my_cli"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"
authors = ["Alice <alice@example.com>"]

[dependencies]
serde = { version = "1.0", features = ["derive"] }
clap = "4.5"
validator_status=pass

verdict=pass

model=gemma-4-31B-it
scenario=SO-13 JSON with Tricky Values
user_turn_1=Generate a JSON object with these exact key-value pairs: empty_string should be "" (an empty string), null_value should be null, zero should be 0, false_value should be false, empty_array should be [], empty_object should be {}, special_chars should be the string containing a backslash, a double quote, a newline, and a tab: `\"\n\t`, nested_null should be an object with key "a" set to null and key "b" set to an array containing null and 1.
assistant_turn_1={
  "empty_string": "",
  "null_value": null,
  "zero": 0,
  "false_value": false,
  "empty_array": [],
  "empty_object": {},
  "special_chars": "\\\"\n\t",
  "nested_null": {
    "a": null,
    "b": [
      null,
      1
    ]
  }
}
final_answer={
  "empty_string": "",
  "null_value": null,
  "zero": 0,
  "false_value": false,
  "empty_array": [],
  "empty_object": {},
  "special_chars": "\\\"\n\t",
  "nested_null": {
    "a": null,
    "b": [
      null,
      1
    ]
  }
}
validator_status=pass
validator_summary=Valid JSON with expected values.
verdict=pass
score=100
summary=Valid JSON with expected values.

result.summary: SQL contained the expected table and two inserts.
result.rawLog:
model=gemma-4-31B-it
scenario=SO-05 SQL CREATE + INSERT
user_turn_1=Generate SQL to create a table called "employees" with columns: id (integer, primary key, auto increment), name (varchar 100, not null), department (varchar 50), salary (decimal 10,2), hire_date (date). Then insert 2 rows: "Alice Chen", "Engineering", 95000.00, "2023-06-15" and "Bob Park", "Marketing", 78500.50, "2024-01-10".
assistant_turn_1=CREATE TABLE employees (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    department VARCHAR(50),
    salary DECIMAL(10,2),
    hire_date DATE
);

INSERT INTO employees (name, department, salary, hire_date) VALUES 
('Alice Chen', 'Engineering', 95000.00, '2023-06-15'),
('Bob Park', 'Marketing', 78500.50, '2024-01-10');
final_answer=CREATE TABLE employees (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    department VARCHAR(50),
    salary DECIMAL(10,2),
    hire_date DATE
);

INSERT INTO employees (name, department, salary, hire_date) VALUES 
('Alice Chen', 'Engineering', 95000.00, '2023-06-15'),
('Bob Park', 'Marketing', 78500.50, '2024-01-10');
validator_status=pass
validator_summary=SQL contained the expected table and two inserts.
verdict=pass
score=100
summary=SQL contained the expected table and two inserts.

result.summary: XML document matched the requested namespace and book data.
result.rawLog:
model=gemma-4-31B-it
scenario=SO-09 XML with Namespaces and Attributes
user_turn_1=Generate an XML document representing a book catalog. The root element is "catalog" with namespace "http://example.com/books" and attribute version="2.0". Include 2 books, each with attributes id and lang. Book 1: id="bk101", lang="en", title "Rust Programming", author "Steve Klabnik", price 39.99 with currency attribute "USD". Book 2: id="bk102", lang="ja", title "プログラミングRust", author "Steve Klabnik", price 4500 with currency attribute "JPY". Include an XML declaration.
assistant_turn_1=<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<catalog xmlns="http://example.com/books" version="2.0">
  <book id="bk101" lang="en">
    <title>Rust Programming</title>
    <author>Steve Klabnik</author>
    <price currency="USD">39.99</price>
  </book>
  <book id="bk102" lang="ja">
    <title>プログラミングRust</title>
    <author>Steve Klabnik</author>
    <price currency="JPY">4500</price>
  </book>
</catalog>
final_answer=<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<catalog xmlns="http://example.com/books" version="2.0">
  <book id="bk101" lang="en">
    <title>Rust Programming</title>
    <author>Steve Klabnik</author>
    <price currency="USD">39.99</price>
  </book>
  <book id="bk102" lang="ja">
    <title>プログラミングRust</title>
    <author>Steve Klabnik</author>
    <price currency="JPY">4500</price>
  </book>
</catalog>
validator_status=pass
validator_summary=XML document matched the requested namespace and book data.
verdict=pass
score=100
summary=XML document matched the requested namespace and book data.

result.summary: HTML table used the semantic wrappers and data cells correctly.
result.rawLog:
model=gemma-4-31B-it scenario=SO-12 HTML Table with Semantic Markup user_turn_1=Generate an HTML table showing quarterly revenue. Headers: Quarter, Revenue, Growth. Data: Q1 $1.2M +5%, Q2 $1.4M +16.7%, Q3 $1.1M -21.4%, Q4 $1.8M +63.6%. Use thead, tbody, and th elements properly. Add a caption "2025 Quarterly Revenue". assistant_turn_1=
2025 Quarterly Revenue
Quarter Revenue Growth
Q1 $1.2M +5%
Q2 $1.4M +16.7%
Q3 $1.1M -21.4%
Q4 $1.8M +63.6%
final_answer=
2025 Quarterly Revenue
Quarter Revenue Growth
Q1 $1.2M +5%
Q2 $1.4M +16.7%
Q3 $1.1M -21.4%
Q4 $1.8M +63.6%
validator_status=pass validator_summary=HTML table used the semantic wrappers and data cells correctly. verdict=pass score=100 summary=HTML table used the semantic wrappers and data cells correctly.

result.summary: ICS event included the required calendar properties.
result.rawLog:
model=gemma-4-31B-it
scenario=SO-06 ICS Calendar Event
user_turn_1=Generate an ICS calendar event for a team meeting on April 15, 2026, from 2:00 PM to 3:30 PM Eastern Time. Title: "Q2 Planning Session". Location: "Conference Room B". Description: "Quarterly planning meeting - bring your project updates". Organizer: alice@company.com.
assistant_turn_1=BEGIN:VCALENDAR
VERSION:2.0
PRODID:-//Company//NONSGML Event Calendar//EN
BEGIN:VEVENT
UID:q2-planning-2026@company.com
DTSTAMP:20231027T100000Z
DTSTART;TZID=America/New_York:20260415T140000
DTEND;TZID=America/New_York:20260415T153000
SUMMARY:Q2 Planning Session
DESCRIPTION:Quarterly planning meeting - bring your project updates
LOCATION:Conference Room B
ORGANIZER;CN=Alice:mailto:alice@company.com
END:VEVENT
END:VCALENDAR
final_answer=BEGIN:VCALENDAR
VERSION:2.0
PRODID:-//Company//NONSGML Event Calendar//EN
BEGIN:VEVENT
UID:q2-planning-2026@company.com
DTSTAMP:20231027T100000Z
DTSTART;TZID=America/New_York:20260415T140000
DTEND;TZID=America/New_York:20260415T153000
SUMMARY:Q2 Planning Session
DESCRIPTION:Quarterly planning meeting - bring your project updates
LOCATION:Conference Room B
ORGANIZER;CN=Alice:mailto:alice@company.com
END:VEVENT
END:VCALENDAR
validator_status=pass
validator_summary=ICS event included the required calendar properties.
verdict=pass
score=100
summary=ICS event included the required calendar properties.

user_turn_1=Generate SQL to create a table called "employees" with columns: id (integer, primary key, auto increment), name (varchar 100, not null), department (varchar 50), salary (decimal 10,2), hire_date (date). Then insert 2 rows: "Alice Chen", "Engineering", 95000.00, "2023-06-15" and "Bob Park", "Marketing", 78500.50, "2024-01-10".
assistant_turn_1=CREATE TABLE employees (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    department VARCHAR(50),
    salary DECIMAL(10,2),
    hire_date DATE
);

INSERT INTO employees (name, department, salary, hire_date) VALUES 
('Alice Chen', 'Engineering', 95000.00, '2023-06-15'),
('Bob Park', 'Marketing', 78500.50, '2024-01-10');

user_turn_1=Generate an XML document representing a book catalog. The root element is "catalog" with namespace "http://example.com/books" and attribute version="2.0". Include 2 books, each with attributes id and lang. Book 1: id="bk101", lang="en", title "Rust Programming", author "Steve Klabnik", price 39.99 with currency attribute "USD". Book 2: id="bk102", lang="ja", title "プログラミングRust", author "Steve Klabnik", price 4500 with currency attribute "JPY". Include an XML declaration.
assistant_turn_1=<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<catalog xmlns="http://example.com/books" version="2.0">
  <book id="bk101" lang="en">
    <title>Rust Programming</title>
    <author>Steve Klabnik</author>
    <price currency="USD">39.99</price>
  </book>
  <book id="bk102" lang="ja">
    <title>プログラミングRust</title>
    <author>Steve Klabnik</author>
    <price currency="JPY">4500</price>
  </book>
</catalog>
final_answer=<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
